title: Pleroma dubium
taxonomy: Pleroma dubium is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in the tropical regions of China, India, and Southeast Asia. Pleroma dubium is classified within the genus Pleroma, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "Pleroma" or "Christmas-berry" plants. The classification of Pleroma dubium is further categorized under the order Ericales and the clade Asterids. The specific classification of Pleroma dubium is not well-documented in all sources, but it is generally accepted as a member of the family Ericaceae.

intro: Pleroma dubium, also known as Myrtus communis var. dubia, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and stress, due to its calming effects on the nervous system. The bioactive constituents of Pleroma dubium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Pleroma dubium is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and supplements.
